prevalence.source: European MS epidemiology syntheses / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing due to improved survival and case ascertainment

vulnerablePopulations: patients with high disease activity requiring rapid escalation
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: McDonald criteria; MRI and CSF widely used; structured MS clinic pathways common

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with access to high-efficacy DMTs and multidisciplinary care; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to MRI, CSF testing, and MS specialist services in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Early DMT initiation with escalation strategies; relapse management; rehabilitation and symptom management clinics
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access variability and cost/reimbursement differences across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust MS registries and broad access to modern DMTs in many systems
